videos.title: Introduction To SQLite And SQLiteStudio
videos.author: Relational Databases and SQL for Beginners
videos.description:
Welcome to Introduction to SQLite!
As a powerful database system, SQLite has become a staple in many businesses across the world.
In this video, we'll be going over various SQLite data types and storage classes.
We'll also cover the installation process and demonstrate how to use its command line tools to create, modify, and query a database.
We'll also explore the differences between databases and analyze the space utilization of SQLite databases.
From there, we'll dive into SQLiteStudio, a user-friendly GUI tool that makes database management a breeze.
With SQLiteStudio, we'll show you how to create tables, add data, and even work with data encryption to keep your information safe and secure.
With this knowledge, you'll be well-equipped to tackle any database-related task.

Links related to this video:
https://sqlite.org/index.html
https://sqlitestudio.pl/

SQL scripts and csv files used in video:
https://github.com/RelationalDatabasesForBeginners/IntroductionToSQLiteAndSQLiteStudio

09:29 About SQLite3_Analyzer
10:37 SQLite3_Analyzer Example
11:34 SQLiteStudio Intro
12:17 Installing SQLiteStudio
13:08 Creating Database/Tables With SQLiteStudio
16:54 Adding Data With SQLiteStudio
18:39 About Data Encryption
19:17 Data Encryption Example With SQLiteStudio

Hello Friends Wellcome to my channel 
RTechGyan.....







Today I am Prasent  ....
How to Create the Database Using Sqlite Studio







Please click on the bell Icon
And Subscribe and share my Channel..







So lets start the Create the Database






First  Click on the Add Database
click on the + button------
choose the location Give the name to the folder 
&Save the batabse file

double click on the Demo1 
Rigth click on the create table -----
Give the name of the table is Test---
Add the column 1 is Id----
column 2 is a Name ----
column 3 is a Age 
click on the Rigth button & Save the Data 


After that you can Add the Row 
row 1--------1   Ritu   19
row 2---------2 Isha   20
row 3-------3 sushil 25

now click on the rigth button & save the 
data into database

you can delete the data and rollback the data ....








Thank you for Watching my Video...

See you soon With My Next Video RTechGyan..... with my new video....




By By Friend.....

videos.title: SQLITE | SQLite Studio {賯賵丕毓丿 亘賷丕賳丕鬲 亘丕賱毓乇亘賶}
videos.author: TechPulse Academy
videos.description:
Database Management Systems (DBMS) course is divided into two sub-courses: Level 1 and Level 2. In Level 1, you will learn how to QUERING a DBMS to get the data distributed over multiple relations (tables). This level is sufficient for jobs like Data Analysts, Data Scientists, Researchers, and Developers. In Level 2, you will learn all aspects related to designing the database which is useful for jobs like Data Engineers.

Playlist: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Hv2dVnUwKuI&list=PL73bE5x5W-IDErOUNGLYUXFcw2Tan_BAq
Facebook page: https://www.facebook.com/OLearningO
Facebook Group: https://www.facebook.com/groups/1296343220550686

This is Level 1 and here is the syllabus
---------------------------------------------------------
1- Introduction to Relational Databases
2- Relational Algebra (Implementation tool: Relax)
    |------ Unary Operators (Select - Project - Rename)
    |------ Binary Operators
                |------ Set Operators (Intersect - Union - Minus)
                |------ Join Operators
                            |------ Inner Join (Theta - Equi - Natural)
                            |------ Outer Join (Left - Right - Full)
                |------ Divide By Operator
   |------ Aggregate Functions and Grouping
3- Tuple Relational Calculas
   |------ One variable
   |------ Two or more variables
   |------ Quantifiers
4- DBMSs introduction
5- SQL  (Implementation tool: SQLite)
    |------ DDL (Create - Drop - Alter)
    |------ DML (Insert - Delete - Update - Replace)
    |------ DQL (Select)
              |------ Select - From - Where
              |------ Join
              |------ Aggregate Functions  and Grouping
              |------ Sorting rows
              |------ Set Operators (Intersect - Union - Minus)
              |------ SubQueries
              |------ Quantifiers
              |------ Divide By Implementation
              |------ Case
              |------ Exercises
6- SQLite as an embedded system
   |------  with Java
   |------  with Python
8- SQL Dialect
9- Postgres as Client-Server model
   |------  with Java
   |------  with Python
